titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 714

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=946 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 12:40:59.67492+00 | translation moa=947 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 12:40:59.663423+00

p.cl:
Sciant presentes et futuri quod ego Willelmus de vallibus dedi . et concessi . et hac presenti mea carta con
firmaui deo et Sancto Cutberto de Dunolmo et Sanctis Monachis ibidem deo et Sancto Cutberto seruientibus unum
toftum in golin Scilicet illum toftum quod iacet propinquius crofto Sancti Iohannis hospitalis Ierosolime erga
meridiem . tam in longo quam predictum croftum Sancti Iohannis est latum . et unam percatam terre quam Vdardus pisca
tor tenuit in prefata uilla . Scilicet illam percatam terre que iacet propinquius crofto Willelmi Smalware erga
septemtrionem . et Costeram aquilonis de Swindgthates sicut exitus est de golin usque ad illam magnam petram
que iacet extra Windgthates . erga septemtrionem . et sic sursum sicut terra Sancti Cutberti est perambulata et
mensurata . Volo eciam quod Osmundus de golin et sui heredes teneant illum predictum toftum . et illam
prefatam percatam terre . et costeram prenominatam libere et quiete et honorifice sicut aliqua elemosina liberius et qui
cius tenetur uel prossidetur in toto regno Scocie . reddendo annuatim predictis monachis . quatuor denarii .
ad festum Sancti Michaelis pro omni seruicio .

His Testibus . Roberto de Hotoft . Gregorio de
fentun . Waltero de Lind’ . Ricardo Senz aueir . Ricardo filio Seleburg . Godardo capellano . Gumero capellano .
Symone clerico . Gilberto pincerna de golin . Roberto filio Ade de Driltun .

div.p: Let present and future know that I, William de Vaux, have given, and granted, and by this my present charter have made firm to God and Saint Cuthbert of Durham and the holy monks serving God and Saint Cuthbert there, one toft in Gullane, that is the toft which lies nearest the croft of the hospital of St John of Jerusalem towards the south, as long as the aforesaid croft of Saint John is broad; and one perch of land which Udard the fisherman held in the aforementioned toun, that is the perch of land which lies nearest the croft of William Smallware towards the north, and the northern edge of Swindgthates, as it has gone out from Gullane towards the large stone which lies outside Windgthates, towards the north, and so upwards as the land of Saint Cuthbert was perambulated and measured. I also will that Osmund of Gullane and his heirs should hold the aforesaid toft, and the aforementioned perch of land, and the aforementioned edge, as freely, quitly, and honourably as any alms is most freely and quitly held or possessed in the whole kindgom of Scotland, by rendering annually to the aforesaid monks four pence at the feast of Saint Michael in return for every service. With these witnesses, Robert Hotoft, Gregory of Fenton, Walter of Lindsay, Richard Sans Avoir, Richard son of Seleburh, Godard the chaplain, Gummer the chaplain, Simon the clerk, Gilbert the butler of Gullane, Robert son of Adam of Dirleton.
